common.operatorBlock: {brandName} is operated by {operatorName} ({operatorStatus}), with address at {operatorCountry}. Contact: {contactEmail}.
common.anpcLine: Romanian National Authority for Consumer Protection (ANPC): {anpcUrl}. Alternative dispute resolution (SAL): {anpcSalUrl}. EU Online Dispute Resolution platform: {odrUrl}.

operator.title: 1. Who we are
operator.intro: These Terms and Conditions (the “Terms”) govern your access to and use of {siteUrl} and any related services we provide (together, the “Platform”). The Platform is operated by:

items.name: Operator: {operatorName} ({operatorStatus}).
items.contact: General contact: {contactEmail}. Legal & billing: {legalEmail}. Phone (where applicable): {phone}.

operator.businessInvoices: {operatorName} sells access to courses and digital content as a private individual. If you are a business and require a fiscal invoice issued to your company, please contact {legalEmail} before or shortly after purchase with your company name, fiscal code, registered address and the order reference.

content.welcome: Welcome to {brandName}. By creating an account, browsing the Platform, purchasing a course or subscribing to a plan, you confirm that you have read, understood and agree to be bound by these Terms, our Privacy Policy, our GDPR Notice, our Cookie Policy and our Refund Policy.
content.readCarefully: If you do not agree with any part of these Terms, you must not use the Platform. These Terms form a legally binding agreement between you and {operatorName}.

items.platform: “Platform” — the website {siteUrl}, the underlying software and any related applications and services we provide.
items.user: “User”, “you” — any person who accesses the Platform, with or without an account.
items.content: “Content” — all courses, lessons, video, audio, text, code, downloads, design and other materials made available through the Platform.
items.subscription: “Subscription” — a recurring paid plan that grants access to Content while active.
items.coursesPlan: “Courses Plan” — a Subscription that grants access to all courses and lessons on the Platform but does not include the GitHub Copilot Bonus.
items.copilotPlan: “Copilot Plan” — a Subscription that includes the Courses Plan benefits plus the GitHub Copilot Bonus described in Section 9.
items.copilotBonus: “GitHub Copilot Bonus” — the optional, discretionary access to a GitHub account with GitHub Copilot enabled, provisioned through third-party providers (Microsoft / GitHub) and described in Section 9.
items.introOffer: “Intro Offer” — a discounted price applied automatically to the first N billing periods of a new Subscription, after which the regular price applies.

content.age: You must be at least 16 years old to create an account. Users under 18 confirm they have the consent of a parent or legal guardian to use the Platform and to enter into these Terms.
content.registration: When you register, you must provide accurate, current and complete information and keep it up to date. You may register only one personal account.
content.responsibility: You are responsible for everything that happens under your account, including the confidentiality of your credentials and any activity carried out using them. Notify us immediately at {legalEmail} if you suspect unauthorised use.
content.suspension: We may suspend, restrict or terminate your account if you breach these Terms, applicable law, or if your activity poses a security or reputational risk to the Platform or other users.

userConduct.title: 5. Acceptable use
userConduct.intro: When using the Platform you agree not to:
prohibitedActions: Violate any applicable law, regulation or third-party right.
Share, resell, sublicense, broadcast, mirror or otherwise redistribute any Content without our prior written consent.
Share your account credentials or allow more than one person to use a single account.
Reverse-engineer, scrape, harvest or systematically copy any part of the Platform or Content.
Upload viruses, malware, or anything intended to disrupt or compromise the Platform.
Attempt to gain unauthorised access to accounts, systems, networks, or non-public areas of the Platform.
Harass, threaten, defame or impersonate any person, or post unlawful, hateful, sexually explicit, or infringing content.
Use automated tools (bots, scripts, agents) to access the Platform in a way that places an unreasonable load on our infrastructure.
Use the Platform or any Content to train, fine-tune or develop a competing AI model, dataset or product without our prior written consent.

content.ownership: All Content on the Platform — including courses, lessons, video, audio, text, code samples, slides, graphics, logos and the underlying software — is owned by {operatorName} or licensed to us, and is protected by copyright, trademark and other applicable intellectual property laws.
content.license: Subject to your compliance with these Terms and payment of any applicable fees, we grant you a limited, personal, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable, revocable licence to access and view the Content for your own personal, non-commercial learning. No other rights are granted, expressly or by implication. You may not download, copy, redistribute, publicly display or create derivative works from the Content without our prior written consent.
content.feedback: If you send us feedback, suggestions, or ideas about the Platform or Content, you grant us a worldwide, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable licence to use them without restriction or compensation.

content.individualPurchases: Some courses can be purchased individually for one-time payment. Such purchases grant you the licence described in Section 6 for as long as we make the course available on the Platform.
content.subscriptionAccess: An active Subscription grants access to the courses and features included in the relevant plan, for as long as the Subscription is active and paid up.
content.billingCycle: Subscriptions renew automatically at the end of each billing period (monthly or yearly, as selected) at the then-current price for that plan, until cancelled. You can cancel at any time from your account; cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period and you keep access until then.
content.pricesAndTaxes: All prices are displayed on the Platform in the currency shown at checkout (typically RON). Prices are inclusive of any applicable taxes unless stated otherwise. Currency conversions and any bank fees are your responsibility.
content.paymentProcessor: Payments are processed by Stripe Payments Europe, Ltd. We do not store your full card details on our servers. By paying, you agree to Stripe's terms and privacy policy.
content.noFiscalInvoiceByDefault: Because the Platform is operated by a private individual, fiscal invoices are not issued automatically. If you require a fiscal invoice (for example as a business client), email {legalEmail} with your company details and the order reference.

introOffers.title: 8. Intro Offers, promotions and price changes

content.introOffer: We may offer an Intro Offer (for example, a lower price for the first month) on certain plans. If you subscribe while an Intro Offer is active, the discounted price applies only to the first N billing periods stated at checkout. After that, the Subscription automatically renews at the regular published price for that plan, until cancelled.
content.noStacking: Intro Offers and other promotional codes cannot be combined unless we expressly state otherwise.
content.priceChangesNew: We may change the price of any plan at any time for new subscribers, including the price applicable after an Intro Offer ends. Changes take effect from the date the updated price is published on the Platform.
content.priceChangesExisting: For existing subscribers, we will give reasonable notice (at least 30 days, sent by email or shown in your account) before any price increase takes effect. You may cancel your Subscription before the new price applies; if you do not cancel, your Subscription will renew at the new price.

content.natureOfBonus: The Copilot Plan currently includes a discretionary, value-added bonus: at our sole discretion we may automatically provision a personal GitHub account with GitHub Copilot enabled, and make the credentials available to you in your profile under Profile → GitHub. This is referred to as the “GitHub Copilot Bonus”.
content.thirdParty: GitHub Copilot is a service provided by GitHub, Inc. and Microsoft Corporation. We are not GitHub or Microsoft, we do not control these services, and we make no representations or warranties on their behalf. The GitHub Copilot Bonus is delivered through these third-party providers and is at all times subject to their own terms of service, acceptable use policies, privacy policies and product changes.
content.personalUseOnly: The provisioned GitHub account is licensed to you personally for non-commercial coding-assistance use within editors such as Visual Studio Code (Insiders recommended) and OpenClaw. You agree to comply with GitHub's Terms of Service and Acceptable Use Policies. You are solely responsible for safeguarding the credentials and for all activity under the provisioned account.
content.noWarranty: The GitHub Copilot Bonus is provided “as available” and on an “as is” basis. We do not warrant that any specific Copilot feature, model, quota, billing arrangement, account type, or third-party service level will be available, or will continue to be available, on any specific date. Available models (including but not limited to Claude Opus, GPT and Gemini), usage limits and feature scope may change at any time at the discretion of the third-party providers.

discretionaryChanges.title: 9.1 Changes to the Copilot Bonus
discretionaryChanges.content: We may, at any time and at our sole discretion, modify, reduce, replace, suspend or completely discontinue the GitHub Copilot Bonus, in whole or in part, including (without limitation): changing the type of account provisioned, the available models, the included quota, or the eligibility criteria. For new subscriptions, any such change takes effect from the date these Terms are updated and published on the Platform. For existing Copilot Plan subscribers, we will give reasonable advance notice (at least 30 days where reasonably possible) before a material reduction or discontinuation takes effect, except where immediate change is required for legal, security, fraud-prevention or third-party-driven reasons (for example, GitHub or Microsoft modifying or terminating the underlying service).

autoDowngrade.title: 9.2 Automatic switch to the Courses Plan
autoDowngrade.content: If we permanently discontinue the GitHub Copilot Bonus, or if we are no longer able to provide it for reasons outside our reasonable control, your Copilot Plan Subscription will automatically convert, on its next renewal date, to the Courses Plan at the then-current published Courses Plan price. You will retain uninterrupted access to all courses and lessons. We will notify you of the conversion in advance where reasonably possible. If you do not wish to continue on the Courses Plan, you may cancel your Subscription before the conversion date and you will not be charged the new amount.

noLiability.title: 9.3 No liability for the Copilot Bonus or third-party services
noLiability.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we shall not be liable for any loss, damage, claim, cost or expense arising out of or in connection with: (a) any unavailability, downtime, slowdown, error, defect, bug, change, suspension, restriction, cancellation or termination of GitHub, GitHub Copilot, Microsoft, Azure or any other third-party service used to deliver the Copilot Bonus; (b) any change in pricing, billing, plans, models, quotas, features, or terms by GitHub or Microsoft; (c) any suspension, restriction or termination of the provisioned GitHub account by GitHub or Microsoft (including for breach of their terms by you or by a third party); (d) any loss of, or inability to access, code, repositories, settings, history or any other data stored on or associated with the provisioned GitHub account; (e) any decision by us to modify, reduce, replace or discontinue the GitHub Copilot Bonus, in whole or in part, in accordance with Section 9.1; or (f) any tax, customs or other regulatory consequences for you arising from receiving the Copilot Bonus.

termination.title: 9.4 What happens to the GitHub account on cancellation
termination.content: If your Subscription is cancelled, expires, is refunded or is terminated for breach, the GitHub Copilot licence associated with the provisioned account may be revoked and the GitHub account itself may be suspended, reclaimed or deleted by us or by the third-party providers. We make no guarantee that data stored on the provisioned account will remain accessible after such termination — back up anything you wish to keep.

content.grant: Some areas of the Platform may allow you to post content (for example, reviews, comments, forum posts or homework submissions). By posting, you grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, sublicensable, perpetual and irrevocable licence to use, reproduce, modify, adapt, publish, translate, create derivative works from, distribute and display such content in connection with operating and promoting the Platform.
content.warranty: You represent and warrant that you own or have all necessary rights to the content you post and that it does not infringe the rights of any third party, contain unlawful content, or violate these Terms.
content.moderation: We may remove, edit or refuse to publish any user content at our discretion, in particular where we reasonably believe it is unlawful, infringing, misleading, harmful, off-topic or otherwise contrary to these Terms — without prior notice.

withdrawal.title: 11. Right of withdrawal — digital content (consumers in the EU)

content.intro: If you are a consumer resident in the European Union or the European Economic Area, you normally have a 14-day right to withdraw from a distance contract under Romanian Law transposing Directive 2011/83/EU (OUG 34/2014).
content.digitalContentWaiver: However, the Content sold on the Platform consists of digital content delivered other than on a tangible medium. Under Article 16(m) of Directive 2011/83/EU and the corresponding provisions of OUG 34/2014, by purchasing a course or activating a Subscription you expressly request that we make the Content immediately available to you and you expressly consent to the immediate performance of the contract. You acknowledge that, as a consequence, you lose your 14-day right of withdrawal once you start accessing any of the Content.
content.checkout: This consent and acknowledgment are confirmed at checkout when you tick the corresponding box (or by completing the purchase, where ticking is not required by your jurisdiction). Until you have accessed any Content for the first time, you may still withdraw within 14 days by emailing {legalEmail} with your order reference.
content.outsideEU: If you are not an EU/EEA consumer, the right of withdrawal under EU law does not apply, but our Refund Policy below may still grant you a remedy.

content.summary: Our full Refund Policy is available at {siteUrl}/refund-policy. In summary, refunds are at our discretion and are typically considered only when: (a) the Content has not been accessed; (b) there is a substantial defect that we cannot reasonably fix in a timely manner; or (c) refunds are required by applicable law.
content.subscriptions: For Subscriptions, you may cancel at any time and you keep access until the end of the current billing period. Already-paid amounts are not refunded on a pro-rata basis except where required by law.
content.introOfferRefunds: Refunds are calculated on the amount actually paid, including any Intro Offer discount you benefited from.

thirdParty.title: 13. Third-party services and links
thirdParty.content: The Platform integrates or links to third-party services, including (without limitation) Stripe (payments), Firebase / Google Cloud (authentication, hosting, storage), Sentry (error monitoring), GitHub and Microsoft (Copilot Bonus delivery), and analytics providers. We are not responsible for, and provide no warranties regarding, the content, availability, security, privacy practices or terms of any third-party service.

availability.title: 14. Availability and changes to the Platform
availability.content: We strive to keep the Platform available, but we do not guarantee uninterrupted access. We may, at any time and without prior notice, modify, suspend or discontinue any part of the Platform or any Content, including individual courses or lessons, for maintenance, security, legal or commercial reasons. We will not be liable to you or any third party for any such modification, suspension or discontinuation.

content.asIs: To the maximum extent permitted by law, the Platform and the Content are provided “as is” and “as available”, without warranties of any kind, whether express, implied or statutory, including (without limitation) warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, accuracy, reliability or uninterrupted operation.
content.noResults: We do not warrant that following a course will lead to any specific learning outcome, employment, certification, income or career result. The educational content provided is informational and reflects the views and experience of the instructor at the time of recording.
content.noProfessionalAdvice: Nothing on the Platform constitutes legal, financial, medical, tax or other professional advice. You should consult a qualified professional before acting on any information.

content.general: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, we shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, exemplary or punitive damages, including loss of profits, revenue, data, goodwill or business opportunities, arising out of or in connection with your use of the Platform or any Content, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages.
content.cap: Without limiting the foregoing, our total aggregate liability to you in any 12-month period shall not exceed the amounts you actually paid to us during that 12-month period for the service that gives rise to the claim, or 100 RON, whichever is higher.
content.consumerCarveOut: Nothing in these Terms excludes or limits our liability where it would be unlawful to do so under applicable consumer-protection law, including liability for death or personal injury caused by our negligence, for fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation, or for the statutory rights of consumers that cannot be waived by contract.

indemnification.title: 17. Indemnification
indemnification.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law, you agree to defend, indemnify and hold harmless {operatorName} and any persons acting on our behalf from and against all claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or related to: (a) your breach of these Terms; (b) your use of the Platform or any Content in breach of applicable law or third-party rights; (c) any content you post on the Platform; or (d) your use of the GitHub Copilot Bonus or the provisioned GitHub account.

content.byYou: You may stop using the Platform and close your account at any time by contacting {legalEmail}.
content.byUs: We may suspend or terminate your access to the Platform, with or without notice, for any breach of these Terms, applicable law, payment failure, or other legitimate reason. Upon termination your licence to access the Content immediately ends.
content.survival: Sections relating to intellectual property, user content licence, disclaimers, limitation of liability, indemnification, governing law, dispute resolution and these termination provisions survive any termination of these Terms.

forceMajeure.title: 19. Force majeure
forceMajeure.content: We are not liable for any delay or failure to perform any obligation under these Terms caused by events beyond our reasonable control, including but not limited to acts of God, war, terrorism, civil unrest, pandemic, government action, labour disputes, internet or telecommunications outages, third-party service failures (including those affecting Stripe, Firebase, Google Cloud, GitHub or Microsoft), cyber-attacks or natural disasters.

changes.title: 20. Changes to these Terms
changes.content: We may amend these Terms from time to time. The current version is always published at {siteUrl}/terms-conditions with the “Last updated” date. Material changes will be communicated by email and/or by an in-product notice at least 15 days before they take effect, except where a shorter period is required for legal, security or third-party-driven reasons. Your continued use of the Platform after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms; if you do not agree, you must stop using the Platform and may cancel your Subscription.

law.title: 21. Governing law and jurisdiction
law.content: These Terms are governed by the laws of {operatorCountry}. Mandatory consumer-protection laws of your country of residence in the EU/EEA continue to apply. Any dispute arising out of or in connection with these Terms shall be submitted to the competent courts of {operatorCountry}, without prejudice to your right, as a consumer, to bring proceedings in the courts of your country of residence.

anpc.title: 22. Consumer protection authorities and dispute resolution

content.anpc: If you are a consumer and have a complaint, please first contact us at {legalEmail} so we can try to resolve it. You may also contact the Romanian National Authority for Consumer Protection (ANPC) at {anpcUrl}.
content.sal: Alternative Dispute Resolution (SAL) for consumer disputes in Romania: {anpcSalUrl}.
content.odr: EU Online Dispute Resolution platform (for cross-border consumer disputes): {odrUrl}.

content.severability: If any provision of these Terms is found to be invalid or unenforceable, that provision will be modified to the minimum extent necessary or severed, and the remaining provisions will continue in full force.
content.noWaiver: Failure to enforce any right under these Terms is not a waiver of that right.
content.assignment: You may not assign or transfer your rights under these Terms without our prior written consent. We may assign our rights to any successor or in connection with a transfer of the Platform.
content.entireAgreement: These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy, GDPR Notice, Cookie Policy, Refund Policy, Legal Notice and DSA Notice, constitute the entire agreement between you and us with respect to the Platform and supersede any prior agreements.
content.language: These Terms are made available in English and Romanian. In case of discrepancy, the Romanian version prevails for users resident in Romania; the English version prevails for all other users.

content.welcome: This Privacy Policy explains how {operatorName} (the operator of {brandName} at {siteUrl}) collects, uses, shares and protects your personal data when you use the Platform.
content.readCarefully: Please read this Policy together with our GDPR Notice (which sets out your rights and our legal bases in detail), our Cookie Policy and our Terms and Conditions.

controller.title: Data controller
controller.content: The data controller is {operatorName}, {operatorStatus}, {operatorCountry}. For any privacy-related request, contact {dpoEmail}.

personalInformation.title: Personal information
personalInformation.intro: We collect personal information that you provide directly to us, in the following situations:
situations: When you create an account, log in, or update your profile.
When you purchase a course or subscribe to a plan.
When you request a fiscal invoice as a business client.
When you contact us by email or other channels.
When you submit reviews, comments or other content on the Platform.

types.intro: Categories of personal data we typically collect:
items: Identity data (name, display name, profile picture).
Contact data (email address, optional phone number).
Account data (Firebase UID, hashed credentials, authentication providers).
Transaction data (payment status, plan, currency, country, last 4 digits of card via Stripe — we do not store full card details).
Billing data for B2B invoices when you request one (company name, fiscal code, registered address).
Optional data you submit (reviews, comments, homework submissions, support messages).

usageInformation.title: Usage information
usageInformation.intro: We automatically collect certain technical and usage data when you interact with the Platform:
items: Device information (device type, operating system, browser).
IP address (used for security, fraud prevention and rough geolocation).
Pages visited, lessons watched, course progress and completion.
Referring URL and search terms.
Performance and error data (via Sentry) to diagnose problems.
Aggregated analytics (Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity, Meta Pixel, TikTok Pixel — only with your cookie consent).
GitHub Copilot Bonus provisioning logs (Copilot Plan only): username assigned, organisation membership status, account health checks.

howWeUseInformation.title: How we use your information
howWeUseInformation.intro: We use your personal data only for the purposes set out below, on the legal bases described in our GDPR Notice:
purposes: To create and operate your account and authenticate you securely.
To deliver the courses, Subscriptions and other features you have purchased.
To provision and manage the GitHub Copilot Bonus when you are on the Copilot Plan.
To process payments, manage Subscriptions and detect or prevent fraud.
To issue fiscal invoices to business clients on request.
To respond to your support and contact requests.
To send service-related communications (account, billing, security, terms updates).
To improve the Platform and our content based on aggregated, non-identifying analytics.
To comply with legal, accounting and regulatory obligations and to defend or assert legal claims.

cookiesAndTracking.title: Cookies and tracking
cookiesAndTracking.intro: We use cookies and similar technologies. See our dedicated Cookie Policy at {siteUrl}/cookie-policy for the full list and your choices.
cookiesAndTracking.typesIntro: The cookies we use fall into the following categories:

types.essential: Strictly necessary — required to operate the Platform (authentication, session, security).
types.functionality: Functionality — remember your preferences such as language and theme.
types.analytics: Analytics — help us understand how the Platform is used (set only with your consent).
types.marketing: Marketing — used to measure the effectiveness of our advertising campaigns (set only with your consent).

cookiesAndTracking.browserSettings: You can manage cookies in your browser settings; refusing non-essential cookies will not prevent you from using core Platform functionality.

dataSharing.title: How we share your data
dataSharing.intro: We share personal data only with the following categories of recipients, under appropriate legal safeguards:

situations.businessPartners: Business partners — instructors and content providers strictly to deliver the Content you have purchased.
situations.serviceProviders: Service providers acting as our processors — including Stripe (payments), Firebase / Google Cloud (authentication, database, hosting, storage), Sentry (error monitoring), Microsoft / GitHub (Copilot Bonus provisioning), Resend or similar (transactional email), and analytics providers where you have consented.
situations.affiliates: Persons acting on our behalf for accounting, legal or tax purposes, bound by confidentiality.
situations.legalRequirements: Public authorities and law enforcement, where required by law or to protect rights, property or safety.
situations.protectionOfRights: Where necessary to investigate, prevent or address fraud, security incidents or breaches of these Terms.
situations.businessTransfers: In the event of a sale, merger or transfer of all or part of the Platform, in which case we will notify you in advance and ensure equivalent protection.

dataSecurity.title: Data security
dataSecurity.content: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your data, including encryption in transit, access controls, audit logs, secret rotation and regular security reviews. No system is 100% secure: in case of a personal data breach affecting your rights, we will notify you and the {dpaName} as required by GDPR.

dataRetention.title: Data retention
dataRetention.content: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary for the purposes set out above, including legal, accounting and reporting requirements. Account and transaction data are typically retained for the duration of your account and up to 10 years after the last transaction for accounting purposes. You may request deletion of data that is no longer required by emailing {dpoEmail}.

yourRights.title: Your rights
yourRights.intro: Subject to applicable law (notably GDPR), you have the following rights:
rights: Access — obtain a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
Rectification — correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
Erasure — request deletion where there is no overriding legal reason for us to keep it.
Restriction — limit how we process your data in certain situations.
Portability — receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Object — object to processing based on our legitimate interests, including direct marketing.
Withdraw consent — at any time, where processing is based on your consent.
yourRights.exercise: To exercise any of these rights, email {dpoEmail}. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with {dpaName} ({dpaUrl}).

childrensPrivacy.title: Children's privacy
childrensPrivacy.content: The Platform is not intended for users under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe we have collected such data, contact {dpoEmail} and we will delete it without undue delay.

changesToPolicy.title: Changes to this Policy
changesToPolicy.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The current version, with the “Last updated” date, is always available at {siteUrl}/privacy-policy. Material changes will be communicated by email or in-product notice.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.paragraph1: This GDPR Notice complements our Privacy Policy and explains how we comply with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) when processing personal data of users in the EU/EEA.
introduction.paragraph2: Capitalised terms not defined here have the meaning given in our Terms and Conditions or Privacy Policy.

dataController.title: Data controller
dataController.intro: The data controller for personal data processed via the Platform is {operatorName} ({operatorStatus}), {operatorCountry}.

categories.title: Categories of personal data
categories.intro: We process the following categories of personal data:
categories.identityData: Identity data — name, display name, profile picture.
categories.contactData: Contact data — email address, optional phone, billing details.
categories.financialData: Financial / transaction data — payment status, amount, currency, last 4 digits of card via Stripe.
categories.technicalData: Technical data — IP address, device, browser, operating system.
categories.profileData: Profile data — preferences, language, theme, learning progress.
categories.usageData: Usage data — pages visited, lessons watched, time spent, feature interactions.
categories.marketingData: Marketing data — only with your explicit consent for analytics and ad measurement.
categories.copilotData: Copilot Bonus data (Copilot Plan only) — provisioned GitHub username, organisation membership status, provisioning timestamps.

collection.title: How we collect personal data
collection.directInteractions: Directly from you, when you register, log in, purchase, contact us or post content.
collection.automatedTechnologies: Automatically, via cookies, server logs and analytics tools where you have consented.
collection.thirdParties: From third-party services (Stripe for payment status, Firebase Auth for authentication, GitHub / Microsoft for Copilot account provisioning state, Google / Meta / TikTok for ad measurement when consented).

legalBasis.title: Legal bases for processing
legalBasis.intro: We process personal data on one or more of the following legal bases (Art. 6 GDPR):
legalBasis.consent: Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) — for non-essential cookies, marketing and analytics where required.
legalBasis.contract: Performance of a contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) — to operate your account, deliver courses, manage your Subscription and provision the Copilot Bonus.
legalBasis.legalObligation: Compliance with a legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) — for accounting, tax, anti-fraud and consumer protection obligations.
legalBasis.legitimateInterests: Our legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f)) — for service improvement, security, fraud prevention, defence of legal claims and direct communications to existing customers about similar services.

purposes.title: Purposes of processing
purposes.intro: We process personal data for the following purposes:
items: Account creation, authentication and security.
Delivering courses, Subscriptions and the Copilot Bonus.
Payment processing, fraud prevention and accounting.
Customer support and dispute resolution.
Service-related and (with consent) marketing communications.
Aggregated analytics and product improvement.
Compliance with legal, regulatory and tax obligations.
Defending or asserting legal claims.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.paragraph1: We retain personal data only as long as necessary for the purposes for which it was collected, or for as long as required by law (typically up to 10 years for accounting and tax records, and the duration of your account plus a reasonable archival period for everything else).
retention.paragraph2: On account closure, we delete or anonymise personal data that is no longer needed for ongoing legal obligations or to defend legal claims.

sharing.title: Recipients of personal data
sharing.intro: We share personal data only with:
sharing.serviceProviders: Processors acting on our written instructions — Stripe, Google / Firebase, Sentry, Microsoft / GitHub, transactional email providers, analytics providers (only with consent).
sharing.businessPartners: Instructors strictly to deliver Content you have purchased, under confidentiality.
sharing.legalAuthorities: Public authorities, regulators or courts where required by law or in legal proceedings.
sharing.requirements: All processors are bound by data processing agreements that ensure GDPR-equivalent protection.

internationalTransfers.title: International transfers
internationalTransfers.intro: Some of our processors are located outside the EU/EEA (notably in the United States — Stripe, Google / Firebase, Microsoft / GitHub, Sentry, Meta, TikTok). Where personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA, we rely on:
items: European Commission adequacy decisions where applicable (e.g. EU-US Data Privacy Framework participants).
Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission.
Supplementary technical and organisational measures where required.
Your explicit consent in specific cases.

rights.title: Your rights under GDPR
rights.intro: You have the following rights under GDPR:
rights.access: Right of access (Art. 15) — to obtain confirmation of, and a copy of, the personal data we process about you.
rights.rectification: Right to rectification (Art. 16) — to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
rights.erasure: Right to erasure (Art. 17) — to request deletion in certain circumstances.
rights.dataPortability: Right to data portability (Art. 20) — to receive your data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format.
rights.object: Right to object (Art. 21) — to processing based on legitimate interests, including direct marketing.
rights.automatedDecisions: Rights related to automated decision-making (Art. 22) — we do not currently take automated decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects on you.
rights.withdrawConsent: Right to withdraw consent (Art. 7) — at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of processing based on consent before withdrawal.
rights.exercise: To exercise any of these rights, email {dpoEmail}. We will respond within one month, extendable by two further months for complex requests.

security.title: Security
security.paragraph1: We implement technical and organisational measures appropriate to the risk, including encryption in transit (HTTPS), encryption at rest where supported by our processors, access controls, audit logs, secret rotation, security headers and regular reviews.
security.paragraph2: In the event of a personal data breach likely to result in a risk to your rights and freedoms, we will notify you and {dpaName} as required by Art. 33 and 34 GDPR.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.content: See our dedicated Cookie Policy at {siteUrl}/cookie-policy for the list of cookies we use and how to manage them.

changes.title: Changes to this Notice
changes.content: We may update this GDPR Notice. The current version is always available at {siteUrl}/gdpr. Material changes will be communicated by email or in-product notice.

complaints.title: Complaints
complaints.content: If you believe we have not handled your personal data properly, please contact {dpoEmail} first so we can try to resolve the issue. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with {dpaName} ({dpaUrl}) or with the supervisory authority in your country of residence.

intro.title: What are cookies?
intro.content: Cookies are small text files placed on your device by websites you visit. They are widely used to make sites work, work more efficiently, and provide reporting information. Similar technologies (local storage, pixels, SDKs) are covered by this Policy as well.

operator.title: Who sets cookies on this site
operator.content: {brandName} ({operatorName}, {operatorStatus}, {operatorCountry}) sets first-party cookies on {siteUrl}. Some third-party services we use may also set cookies — see the categories below.

items.essential: Strictly necessary — required for authentication, session management, load balancing and security. Cannot be disabled. Examples: Firebase Auth session, CSRF protection.
items.functionality: Functionality — remember preferences such as language and theme so you do not have to set them again. Set on first interaction, lifetime up to 12 months.
items.analytics: Analytics — help us understand aggregate Platform usage so we can improve it. Set only with your consent. Examples: Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity.
items.marketing: Marketing — used to measure ad performance and (where applicable) personalise content. Set only with your consent. Examples: Meta Pixel, TikTok Pixel.

manage.title: How to manage cookies
manage.content: When you first visit {siteUrl}, our cookie banner asks for your consent for non-essential cookies. You can change your choice at any time by clearing your browser's site data for {siteUrl} and reloading the page, or by using your browser's cookie controls. Refusing non-essential cookies does not prevent you from using core Platform functionality.

doNotTrack.title: Do Not Track
doNotTrack.content: We do not currently respond to “Do Not Track” browser signals because no consistent industry standard has been adopted. We respect your choice via our cookie banner and your browser controls.

changes.title: Changes to this Policy
changes.content: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. The current version is always available at {siteUrl}/cookie-policy.

intro.title: Overview
intro.content: This Refund Policy applies to one-time course purchases and Subscriptions sold through {siteUrl}. It supplements (and does not replace) the rights you have under applicable consumer-protection law.

digitalContent.title: Digital content — right of withdrawal
digitalContent.content: Courses and Subscriptions are digital content delivered other than on a tangible medium. As described in Section 11 of our Terms and Conditions, by purchasing you expressly request immediate performance and acknowledge that you lose the 14-day right of withdrawal under Romanian Law transposing Directive 2011/83/EU once you start accessing any of the Content. If you have not yet accessed any Content, you can request withdrawal within 14 days by emailing {legalEmail}.

discretionaryRefunds.title: Discretionary refunds
discretionaryRefunds.intro: Outside of the statutory right above, refunds are at our discretion and are typically considered when one or more of the following applies:
items: You have not accessed any Content from the order in question.
There is a substantial defect in the Content that we cannot reasonably fix in a timely manner.
A duplicate or accidental charge occurred (we will refund the duplicate amount).
Refunds are required by applicable law in your country of residence.

subscriptions.title: Subscriptions
subscriptions.content: You can cancel a Subscription at any time from your account. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period and you keep access until then. We do not refund already-paid amounts on a pro-rata basis except where required by law (for example, for material defects we cannot remedy).

introOffers.title: Intro Offers
introOffers.content: If you cancel during an Intro Offer period (for example, the discounted first month), you keep access until the end of that period and are not charged the regular price. Refunds, where granted, are calculated on the actual amount paid (i.e. the discounted Intro amount, not the regular price).

copilotBonus.title: GitHub Copilot Bonus
copilotBonus.content: If we change, reduce or discontinue the GitHub Copilot Bonus, your Subscription will automatically convert to the Courses Plan as described in Section 9.2 of the Terms. Such a conversion does not by itself entitle you to a refund of amounts already paid; you may, of course, cancel your Subscription before the conversion date and you will not be charged the new amount.

howToRequest.title: How to request a refund
howToRequest.intro: Email {legalEmail} from the address associated with your account, including:
items: Your full name.
The order or Subscription reference.
The reason for the refund request.
Any supporting evidence.
howToRequest.timing: We aim to respond within 5 business days and to process approved refunds within 14 days, using the original payment method.

chargebacks.title: Chargebacks
chargebacks.content: If you initiate a chargeback through your bank or card issuer instead of contacting us, we may suspend your account while the dispute is open. Initiating a chargeback for a charge that was correctly authorised may be considered a breach of these Terms.

items.anpc: Romanian National Authority for Consumer Protection (ANPC): {anpcUrl}.
items.sal: Alternative Dispute Resolution (SAL) for consumer disputes: {anpcSalUrl}.

hosting.title: Hosting and infrastructure
hosting.content: The Platform is hosted by Vercel Inc. and Google Cloud Platform (Firebase). Payments are processed by Stripe Payments Europe, Ltd.

ip.title: Intellectual property
ip.content: All content on {siteUrl}, including courses, lessons, code samples, graphics, logos and software, is owned by {operatorName} or licensed to us. Unauthorised reproduction or redistribution is prohibited. To report alleged copyright infringement, contact {legalEmail} with details of the work and the allegedly infringing material.

responsibility.title: Editorial responsibility
responsibility.content: Editorial responsibility for the content published on {siteUrl} rests with {operatorName}, contactable at {legalEmail}.

scope.title: Scope
scope.content: This notice complies with our information obligations under Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 (the “Digital Services Act”). The Platform is not a Very Large Online Platform; this notice covers the obligations applicable to small operators.

singleContact.title: Single point of contact (Art. 11 & 12 DSA)
singleContact.content: Single point of contact for authorities, recipients of the service and any other party: {legalEmail}. Communications may be sent in English or Romanian. We accept communication electronically; postal address: {operatorCountry}.

legalRepresentative.title: Legal representative
legalRepresentative.content: {operatorName}, {operatorStatus}, established in {operatorCountry} (an EU Member State), so no separate Art. 13 representative is required.

illegalContent.title: Notice and action — reporting illegal content (Art. 16 DSA)
illegalContent.intro: If you believe specific content on the Platform is illegal, send a notice to {legalEmail} including:
items: A sufficiently substantiated explanation why you consider the content illegal.
A clear indication of the exact electronic location (URL) of the content.
Your name and email (not required for notices about content involving sexual offences against minors).
A statement confirming your good-faith belief that the information is accurate and complete.
illegalContent.outro: We will acknowledge receipt and take a decision in a timely, diligent, non-arbitrary and objective manner.

decisions.title: Statement of reasons (Art. 17 DSA)
decisions.content: Where we restrict the visibility of, suspend, or remove your content, suspend or terminate your account, or restrict your ability to monetise content, we will provide a clear and specific statement of reasons including (where applicable) the legal or contractual ground, the territorial scope, and the redress mechanisms available.

internalComplaints.title: Internal complaint-handling (Art. 20 DSA, where applicable)
internalComplaints.content: If you disagree with a decision we have taken under Art. 17, you may submit a complaint to {legalEmail} within 6 months of the decision. We will review complaints individually, in a timely, non-discriminatory and diligent manner, by a person who did not take the original decision.

outOfCourt.title: Out-of-court dispute settlement
outOfCourt.content: Recipients in the EU may also use out-of-court dispute settlement bodies certified under Art. 21 DSA in their Member State, the Romanian ANPC SAL ({anpcSalUrl}) for consumer disputes, or the EU ODR platform ({odrUrl}).

trustedFlaggers.title: Trusted flaggers
trustedFlaggers.content: Notices submitted by entities awarded the status of “trusted flagger” under Art. 22 DSA in any EU Member State are treated with priority.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.content: We do not currently meet the thresholds requiring publication of detailed transparency reports under Art. 15 DSA. Should those thresholds be reached, the corresponding reports will be published on this page.
